replication_study.hypothesis: In a task-switching paradigm with within-subject reward manipulation (rewardlevel: -1=low, 1=high), larger rewards will differentially affect reaction times on switch versus repeat trials as a function of individual Need for Cognition (NFC): the three-way interaction NFC × trial (switch=1 vs repeat=-1) × rewardlevel will be positive when predicting standardized log reaction times on correct trials, controlling for experimental block and subject random intercepts.

data_plan.source_type: Laboratory/online experiment dataset with trial-level RT/accuracy and questionnaire measures (NFC, BIS/BAS, etc.)
data_plan.access_details: CSV provided for replication at /app/data/sandra_replicate.csv; variable definitions in sandra_replicate_csv_codebook.pdf.

qualification.explanation: This dataset reproduces the original experimental structure (task-switching with within-subject reward manipulation; trial-level RT/accuracy; participant-level NFC and EF measures) with a new, larger independent sample. It thus provides a distinct sample while maintaining high fidelity to the original measures and design, enabling a robust test of the focal three-way interaction.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Both datasets implement a task-switching paradigm with reward manipulation and analyze log-transformed RTs on correct trials via mixed-effects models including trial type, reward level, block, and individual differences (NFC/EF), as stated in original_paper.pdf (Methods: Data analysis; pp. 103–104) and summarized in post_registration.json (method.models, method.outcome_variable). The replication dataset includes: trial (repeat=-1/switch=1), rewardlevel (-1 low, 1 high), blocknumber (contrast-coded), NFC scores, Stroopindex, and per-trial logRT and accuracy (sandra_replicate_csv_codebook.pdf).
qualification.deviation_from_original: The replication dataset computes Stroopindex as logRTcongruent - logRTincongruent (codebook), which is the sign-reverse (and not standardized) of the EF cost described in the original (post_registration.json notes: standardized RT difference incongruent − congruent). This sign difference will invert expected coefficient signs for EF unless accounted for. The replication sample is substantially larger (127 participants vs. original NFC analyses N=43; post_registration.json) and may include different recruitment channels (codebook references attention checks and gender coding including non-binary), potentially reflecting an online or broader lab population. Also, accuracy models are approximated by GEE in Python (see planned_method) rather than GLMM (lme4) used originally (original_paper.pdf; analysis syntax PDF).

data_plan.notes: Key encodings: trial is contrast-coded (-1 repeat, 1 switch), rewardlevel is contrast-coded (-1 low, 1 high), blocknumber contrast-coded. RT analyses should subset to accuracy==1 and use logRT. The dataset includes precomputed centered variables (HiNFC, LoNFC, HiStroopindex, LoStroopindex), but primary models use standardized continuous predictors. Minor missingness in 'trial' exists (35306 non-null vs 35560 rows); listwise deletion will apply. EF index sign differs from original definition; interpretation of EF three-way sign should account for this.

planned_method.steps: 1) Load /app/data/sandra_replicate.csv. 2) For RT analyses, subset to accuracy==1. 3) Standardize logRT within the analysis sample; standardize NFC and Stroopindex. 4) Fit mixed-effects linear model: logRT_z ~ z(NFC) * trial * rewardlevel + blocknumber + (1|SubjectID). 5) Fit analogous model substituting z(Stroopindex) for z(NFC). 6) Optionally, fit accuracy models via GEE (binomial) with exchangeable correlation by SubjectID: accuracy ~ z(NFC) * trial * rewardlevel + blocknumber; and similarly for z(Stroopindex). 7) Save coefficient estimates, p-values, and summaries to /app/data. 8) Compare focal three-way interaction signs and significance to the original paper.
planned_method.models: Mixed-effects linear regression for RT (random intercept for SubjectID). For accuracy, generalized estimating equations (binomial family, exchangeable correlation by SubjectID) as an approximation to GLMM.
planned_method.outcome_variable: Standardized log reaction time (logRT_z) on correct trials for RT models; trial-level accuracy (0/1) for optional accuracy models.
planned_method.independent_variables: z(NFC) or z(Stroopindex) interacted with trial (−1 repeat, 1 switch) and rewardlevel (−1 low, 1 high). Blocknumber as a fixed-effect covariate.
planned_method.control_variables: Blocknumber; subject-level random intercepts in RT models. Note: For accuracy, correlation handled via GEE exchangeable structure by SubjectID.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10; pandas for data handling; statsmodels for MixedLM and GEE; numpy/scipy for numerics.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Coefficient for the three-way interaction term: z(NFC):trial:rewardlevel (primary) and z(Stroopindex):trial:rewardlevel (secondary).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wald z-test from mixed-effects model (RT) and GEE (accuracy); two-sided alpha=0.05.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion within each model; RT models restricted to rows with accuracy==1.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: No correction for the single focal hypothesis. If reporting both NFC and EF interactions, clearly mark the focal test (NFC three-way) and treat secondary analyses as exploratory or apply Holm–Bonferroni if formal multiplicity control is desired.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Primary success criterion: the NFC × trial × rewardlevel coefficient is positive and statistically significant at p<0.05 (two-sided), consistent with original (β≈0.0382, p=0.008). Secondary: EF (Stroop) three-way is negative and significant; note sign may invert due to Stroopindex sign in this dataset.

files.analysis_sandra_replication__py.py: Python script that loads /app/data/sandra_replicate.csv, standardizes variables, fits mixed-effects linear models for logRT (random intercept for SubjectID) testing the NFC × trial × rewardlevel and Stroop × trial × rewardlevel interactions, approximates accuracy models via GEE, and saves outputs to /app/data/replication_results.json and /app/data/replication_model_summaries.txt.

codebase.notes: All file IO is under /app/data. The script uses statsmodels MixedLM for RT models and GEE (binomial, exchangeable) for accuracy models to approximate the original GLMMs (R lme4). Ensure that sandra_replicate.csv is available at /app/data before execution.

analysis.instructions: 1) Place sandra_replicate.csv in ./data so it is mounted to /app/data inside the container. 2) Build/run the container with the specified base image and packages. 3) Execute: python data/original/34/input/replication_data/analysis_sandra_replication__py.py. 4) Inspect outputs: /app/data/replication_results.json (machine-readable key coefficients) and /app/data/replication_model_summaries.txt (full model summaries).
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare the sign and statistical significance of the NFC × trial × rewardlevel coefficient to the original (expected positive, p<0.05). Optionally compare magnitude (target ≈ 0.038 in standardized logRT units) and the EF (Stroop) three-way (expected negative, noting possible sign inversion due to EF index coding).
